summary: Iteration 4 backend testing complete. 17/17 NEW iter4 tests PASSED for bulk upload automation (bulk-from-videos, preflight, ai-enrich, connect-all-urls + 7 regression smoke). Full suite: 65/67 passed (97%). The 2 failures are pre-existing/order-dependent, NOT introduced by iter4: (1) test_oauth_start_with_client_id — known iter3 behavior change (also documented in iter3 report), (2) test_publish_all — state-pollution fragility in iter1: publish-all queues ALL pending uploads including leftovers from previous test runs, exceeding the test's 5s wait window. New iter4 coverage proves: bulk-from-videos creates one upload per (video_ready song × matching-language channel × format) with strict no-cross-language matching (EN song never maps to ES channel, ES never to EN), dedupes correctly on re-call (created=0), preflight returns empty url for channels with no resolvable client AND populates Google OAuth URL with client_id/state/scope when language matches, ready[] contains channels with refresh_token, ai-enrich deterministic fallback DOES literally embed the global_description text AND the language-derived hashtag (#English, #Spanish) AND pulls title from song.title AND fills tags list, only_empty=true default preserves manual edits, regenerate=true overwrites, connect-all-urls excludes already-connected channels AND excludes channels without resolvable client, all responses are _id-free.

minor.issue: publish-all queues every pending upload globally — including stale ones left by prior test runs/sessions. This makes /api/backend_test.py::test_publish_all order-dependent and flaky in CI. Recommend either: (a) scope publish-all by query param (e.g. project_id), or (b) add DELETE /api/uploads/{id} so tests can isolate, or (c) test should poll-until-published with longer timeout.

Still failing iter1 test_oauth_start_with_client_id — _pick_oauth_client legacy fallback requires both google_client_id AND google_client_secret. Documented in iter3. Action: update iter1 fixture to set both, or relax to OR.

action_items: Optionally fix /api/backend_test.py::test_publish_all by scoping publish-all (project_id filter) OR adding DELETE /api/uploads/{id} so test can isolate (recommended, also useful for UI undo)
Address iter3-documented test_oauth_start_with_client_id by adding google_client_secret to the iter1 fixture (still open)
Consider adding DELETE /api/uploads/{id} and DELETE /api/uploads?project_id=... for test cleanup and user-facing 'cancel queued upload' flows — currently uploads accumulate indefinitely in MongoDB
critical_code_review_comments: server.py:1043-1046 fallback 'if no language match, use ALL channels' is dangerous in production — an Italian-only channel could silently get a Spanish song queued for upload if no Italian channel exists. Recommend: skip the song entirely and surface a warning in the response (e.g. {created, skipped: [{song_id, reason: 'no matching channel'}]}) so the UI can show what was not queued.

server.py:1050 dedupe key is (song_id, channel_id, format) regardless of status — meaning a previously-FAILED or already-PUBLISHED upload prevents re-queuing for the same song/channel. To allow re-publishing after a failure, the dedupe should ignore 'failed' status (e.g. {status: {$in: ['pending','uploading','published']}}).

server.py:1083 preflight WRITES channel.oauth_client_id as a side effect of a GET request — violates REST idempotency. A GET should not mutate state. Move the channel.oauth_client_id assignment into the explicit oauth-start POST or connect-all-urls POST, both of which already do this. The mutation in GET /preflight makes calling preflight twice + adding a new oauth_client in between produce different states.

server.py:1088-1107 _qwen_text swallows ALL exceptions (broad except) and returns '' — debugging real API integration failures will be impossible. Add log.exception or at minimum log.warning(f'qwen failed: {e}') before returning ''.

server.py:1133 ai-enrich condition `if regenerate or only_empty:` always evaluates to True (only_empty defaults to True), so the wrapping `if` is dead code — remove it. The inner `need_*` checks already handle the regenerate-vs-only-fill-empty logic correctly.

server.py:1157 deterministic-fallback tags include `style.lower().split()` words >2 chars but does NOT dedupe — e.g. 'lofi piano' yields tags ['lofi','piano','music video','ai music','lofi piano','lofi','piano'] with duplicates. Add: `tags = list(dict.fromkeys(tags))[:12]`.

server.py:1159 sets chunk['privacy']='public' only when upload row has no privacy — but bulk-from-videos always seeds privacy='public', so this branch is dead. Remove unless ai-enrich is also expected to run on uploads created via POST /uploads with no privacy.

server.py:1144 fallback hashtag `#{style.replace(' ','')}` produces e.g. '#lofipiano' if style='lofi piano' — fine, but if style is empty the hashtag becomes literal '#' which looks broken. Skip empty style hashtag: `(f'#{style.replace(" ","")}' if style else '')`.
server.py is now 1226 lines and the iter3 recommendation to split into modules is overdue (oauth.py, channels.py, uploads.py, ai.py, jobs.py, bible.py). Strongly recommend before iter5.
No pagination on GET /uploads, /jobs, /oauth-clients — `.to_list(2000/500/200)` is a hard cap. With this iter, uploads can grow fast (songs × channels × formats). Add ?limit&skip query params.

success_rate.backend: 97% (65/67) — 17/17 new iter4 + 12/12 iter3 + 15/15 iter2 + 21/23 iter1; the 2 failures are pre-existing and NOT introduced by iter4 changes

test_credentials: none required — openrouter_api_key intentionally left empty to force deterministic fallback path which is what we validate
seed_data_creation: Module-scoped 'seeded' fixture in test_iteration4.py creates: 1 project 'TEST_iter4_proj' + 2 songs (English/Spanish) brought to video_ready via /api/songs/{id}/compose-video (worker takes ~2.4s/job, polled with 12s timeout) + 2 channels (English/Spanish). Teardown deletes the project and channels. NOTE: /api/uploads has no DELETE endpoint, so iter4-created Upload rows persist after test runs.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Run `pytest /app/backend/tests/ -v` for full regression. iter4 tests are in /app/backend/tests/test_iteration4.py (17 tests, all PASS). Key seed: fixture uses /api/songs/{id}/compose-video and polls song.status==video_ready (worker is ~2.4s; 12s polling timeout). The deterministic fallback contract for /api/uploads/ai-enrich is: description MUST literally contain the supplied global_description text AND '#{Language}' hashtag (e.g. '#English', '#Spanish'). Test order matters slightly: TestPreflight.test_preflight_ready... uses /oauth-complete to mark EN channel connected; TestConnectAllUrls then asserts EN does NOT appear in items[]. Both backend_test.py failures (test_oauth_start_with_client_id, test_publish_all) are pre-existing — do not block iter4 sign-off.
rca of the issue: test_publish_all fails because: (1) publish-all queues ALL pending uploads — not just the ones the test created. (2) The DB carries dozens of accumulated pending uploads from prior iter4/iter1 runs (no DELETE /uploads endpoint exists for cleanup). (3) The worker processes uploads via FastAPI BackgroundTasks which run after the response, ~2.4s/job. (4) The test's 5s wait is too short when 10+ uploads are queued. Reproduction: curl POST /api/uploads/publish-all → curl GET /api/uploads → observe 'uploading' status persists >5s. Mitigation options: (a) longer poll loop with timeout (preferred), (b) add DELETE /api/uploads/{id} and have test purge before publish-all, (c) make publish-all accept a list of upload IDs.
